# exportarr

![Version: 3.0.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/Version-3.0.0-informational?style=flat-square) ![Type: application](https://img.shields.io/badge/Type-application-informational?style=flat-square) ![AppVersion: v2.3.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/AppVersion-v2.3.0-informational?style=flat-square)
A Helm Chart to export Prometheus metrics for Arr applications.

A Helm Chart to deploy [exportarr](https://github.com/onedr0p/exportarr), an app that gathers Prometheus metrics from `Arr` instances. See the upstream repository for the full list of supported applications.

This chart can optionally install a Prometheus exporter for the following, as subchart dependencies:
1. `qbittorrent` using the [qbittorrent-exporter](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/qbittorrent-exporter) chart.
2. `Tdarr` using the [tdarr-exporter](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/tdarr-exporter) chart.

This allows you to consolidate different `Arr`-related exporters into a single helm chart. See the [Additional Exporters](#additional-exporters) section for more information.

## Add Chart Repo
```bash
helm repo add homeylab https://homeylab.github.io/helm-charts/
# update the chart, this can also be run to pull new versions of the chart for upgrades
helm repo update homeylab
```

## Prerequisites
Each `Arr` instance you enable under `exportarr.apps.<app>` needs a reachable `url` and, unless the target app has auth disabled, either an inline `apiKey` or an `existingSecret` (the secret's `secretKey` must hold the API key). Multiple instances of the same app (e.g. two `radarr` instances) are supported since `apps.<app>` is a list â€” give each a unique `name`.

If you enable the optional `qbittorrent-exporter` or `tdarr-exporter` subcharts, see their prerequisites in their own charts ([qbittorrent-exporter](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/qbittorrent-exporter), [tdarr-exporter](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/tdarr-exporter)) â€” the [Additional Exporters](#additional-exporters) section below covers passthrough values for both.

## Install
```bash
helm install exportarr homeylab/exportarr -n exportarr --create-namespace

# with own values file - recommended
helm install -f my-values.yaml exportarr homeylab/exportarr -n exportarr --create-namespace
```

### OCI Registry Support
```bash
helm install exportarr -n exportarr oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts/exportarr --version X.Y.Z --create-namespace

# with own values file - recommended
helm install -f my-values.yaml exportarr -n exportarr oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts/exportarr --version X.Y.Z --create-namespace
```

### Install Example
Only some options are shown, view `values.yaml` for an exhaustive list.

<details closed>
<summary>custom-values.yaml</summary>
<br>

```yaml
# custom-values.yaml
exportarr:
  metrics:
    serviceMonitor:
      enabled: true
      additionalLabels:
        app: exportarr
  apps:
    radarr:
    - name: main
      enabled: true
      url: "https://radarr.somedomain/"
      apiKey: "someApiKey" # provide here or `existingSecret` section
    sonarr:
    - name: main
      enabled: true
      url: "https://sonarr.somedomain/"
      apiKey: "someApiKey"
      extraEnv:
        ENABLE_ADDITIONAL_METRICS: true # example specifying extraEnv
    prowlarr:
    - name: main
      enabled: true
      url: "https://prowlarr.somedomain/"
      apiKey: "someApiKey"
    bazarr:
    - name: main
      enabled: true
      url: "https://bazarr.somedomain/"
      apiKey: "someApiKey"

## additional exporters ##
qbittorrent-exporter:
  enabled: false

tdarr-exporter:
  enabled: false
```
</details>
<br>

## Upgrade
```bash
helm upgrade exportarr homeylab/exportarr -n exportarr

# with own values file - recommended
helm upgrade -f my-values.yaml exportarr homeylab/exportarr -n exportarr
```

### OCI Registry Support
```bash
helm upgrade exportarr -n exportarr oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts/exportarr --version X.Y.Z

# with own values file - recommended
helm upgrade -f my-values.yaml exportarr -n exportarr oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts/exportarr --version X.Y.Z
```

### Upgrade Matrix For Releases
_The matrix below displays certain versions of this helm chart that could result in breaking changes._

| Start Chart Version | Target Chart Version | Upgrade Steps |
| ------------------- | -------------------- | ------------- |
| `2.1.X` | `3.0.0` | Backing `qbittorrent-exporter` subchart swapped image/schema, hardened `securityContext` on all exportarr instances, inline `apiKey` now renders into a chart-managed Secret. **Read the [Migrating to 3.0.0](#migrating-to-300) section in full before upgrading.** |
| `1.X.X` | `2.0.0` | `exportarr.apps.<app>` (radarr/sonarr/etc.) changed from an object to a list, allowing multiple instances of the same `Arr` app (e.g. `radarr1`, `radarr2`). `exportarr.testCurlImage` was renamed to `exportarr.testImage`. |

## Migrating to 3.0.0
This version hardens the umbrella chart and swaps the backing image for the optional `qbittorrent-exporter` subchart. Review all items before upgrading.

- **`qbittorrent-exporter` subchart swapped (breaking, only if enabled).** The optional `qbittorrent-exporter` dependency bumped `0.1.0` -> `1.0.0`, moving from the unmaintained `caseyscarborough/qbittorrent-exporter` (Java) to the actively maintained `martabal/qbittorrent-exporter` (Go). Passthrough values under the top-level `qbittorrent-exporter:` key are renamed: `settings.config.base_url` -> `settings.config.baseUrl`, `settings.auth.username`/`password` -> `settings.auth.user`/`pass`. A new `settings.config.insecureSkipVerify` and `settings.auth.apiKey` are also available. **Metric names and labels differ** â€” re-import the Grafana dashboard for the subchart after upgrading. If you enable this subchart, review its own [chart README](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/qbittorrent-exporter) for the full schema.
- **Hardened `securityContext` defaults (breaking).** All `exportarr` instances now run with `runAsNonRoot`, `runAsUser: 65532` (distroless nonroot), `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, and `drop: [ALL]`. Fine for the stock `onedr0p/exportarr` image; override `exportarr.podSecurityContext`/`exportarr.securityContext` if you run a customized one.
- **Credentials render into a chart-managed Secret (behavior-preserving).** Inline `exportarr.apps.<app>[].apiKey` now populates a chart-managed per-instance `Secret`, referenced via `secretKeyRef`, instead of being set as a plaintext environment variable. No action needed â€” `existingSecret` still takes precedence over `apiKey` as before.

## Configuration Options
| Key | Type | Default | Description |
|-----|------|---------|-------------|
| exportarr.affinity | object | `{}` | affinity rules for exportarr pods |
| exportarr.apps | object | `{"bazarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"lidarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"prowlarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"radarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"readarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"sabnzbd":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"sonarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}]}` | per-instance configuration; each key (radarr, sonarr, prowlarr, bazarr, lidarr, sabnzbd, readarr) takes a list of instance objects with fields: name, enabled, url, existingSecret.{name,secretKey}, apiKey, extraEnv, volumes, volumeMounts, metrics |
| exportarr.extraEnv | object | `{}` | extra environment variables applied to all exportarr instances |
| exportarr.fullnameOverride | string | `""` |  |
| exportarr.image.name | string | `"onedr0p/exportarr"` | image repository name |
| exportarr.image.pullPolicy | string | `"IfNotPresent"` | image pull policy |
| exportarr.image.repository | string | `"ghcr.io"` | image repository registry |
| exportarr.image.tag | string | `""` | overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion |
| exportarr.imagePullSecrets | list | `[]` |  |
| exportarr.livenessProbe | object | `{"failureThreshold":5,"httpGet":{"path":"/healthz","port":"metrics"},"initialDelaySeconds":5,"periodSeconds":10,"successThreshold":1,"timeoutSeconds":3}` | liveness probe configuration for the exportarr instances |
| exportarr.metrics.enabled | bool | `true` | enable/disable prometheus podAnnotations and serviceMonitor |
| exportarr.metrics.podAnnotations | object | `{"prometheus.io/path":"/metrics","prometheus.io/port":"9707","prometheus.io/scrape":"true"}` | Add podAnnotations for prometheus scraping |
| exportarr.metrics.podAnnotations."prometheus.io/path" | string | `"/metrics"` | set the path for prometheus scraping |
| exportarr.metrics.podAnnotations."prometheus.io/port" | string | `"9707"` | set the port for prometheus scraping, should match the service port |
| exportarr.metrics.prometheusRule.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable prometheusRule |
| exportarr.metrics.prometheusRule.labels | object | `{}` | set labels for prometheusRule |
| exportarr.metrics.prometheusRule.rules | list | `[]` | Configure additionial rules for the chart under this key. |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.additionalLabels | object | `{}` | set additional labels for serviceMonitor |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable serviceMonitor, if enabled podAnnotations will be ignored |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.interval | string | `"4m"` | how often to scrape for serviceMonitor |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.metricRelabelings | list | `[]` |  |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.path | string | `"/metrics"` | set the path for prometheus scraping for serviceMonitor |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.relabelings | list | `[]` |  |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.scrapeTimeout | string | `"60s"` | scrapeTimeout for serviceMonitor |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.targetLabels | list | `[]` |  |
| exportarr.nameOverride | string | `""` |  |
| exportarr.nodeSelector | object | `{}` | node selector for exportarr pods |
| exportarr.podAnnotations | object | `{}` |  |
| exportarr.podSecurityContext | object | `{"fsGroup":65532,"runAsGroup":65532,"runAsNonRoot":true,"runAsUser":65532,"seccompProfile":{"type":"RuntimeDefault"}}` | pod-level security context applied to all exportarr instances |
| exportarr.readinessProbe | object | `{"failureThreshold":5,"httpGet":{"path":"/healthz","port":"metrics"},"initialDelaySeconds":5,"periodSeconds":10,"successThreshold":1,"timeoutSeconds":2}` | readiness probe configuration for the exportarr instances |
| exportarr.resources | object | `{}` | resource requests/limits for each exportarr deployment |
| exportarr.securityContext | object | `{"allowPrivilegeEscalation":false,"capabilities":{"drop":["ALL"]},"readOnlyRootFilesystem":true}` | container-level security context applied to all exportarr instances |
| exportarr.service.name | string | `"metrics"` | name for the service port |
| exportarr.service.port | int | `9707` | port for exportarr metrics scraping |
| exportarr.service.protocol | string | `"TCP"` | protocol for the service |
| exportarr.service.type | string | `"ClusterIP"` | Kubernetes Service type |
| exportarr.serviceAccount | object | `{"annotations":{},"create":false,"name":""}` | serviceAccount configuration |
| exportarr.startupProbe | object | `{"failureThreshold":5,"httpGet":{"path":"/healthz","port":"metrics"},"initialDelaySeconds":2,"periodSeconds":5,"successThreshold":1,"timeoutSeconds":2}` | startup probe configuration for the exportarr instances |
| exportarr.testImage.name | string | `"busybox"` |  |
| exportarr.testImage.path | string | `"/healthz"` |  |
| exportarr.testImage.pullPolicy | string | `"IfNotPresent"` |  |
| exportarr.testImage.repository | string | `"docker.io"` |  |
| exportarr.testImage.tag | string | `"1.36.1"` |  |
| exportarr.tolerations | list | `[]` | tolerations for exportarr pods |
| exportarr.updateStrategy.type | string | `"Recreate"` | set the update strategy for the deployment |
| exportarr.volumeMounts | list | `[]` |  |
| exportarr.volumes | list | `[]` | additional volumes for all exportarr deployments |
| qbittorrent-exporter.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable the qbittorrent-exporter subchart |
| qbittorrent-exporter.extraEnv | object | `{}` | extra env applied to the qbittorrent-exporter subchart |
| qbittorrent-exporter.metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable serviceMonitor for the qbittorrent-exporter subchart |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.auth.apiKey | string | `""` | qBittorrent API key. Maps to subchart `settings.auth.apiKey` |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.auth.existingSecret | string | `""` | use an existing secret instead of user/pass/apiKey. Maps to subchart `settings.auth.existingSecret` |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.auth.pass | string | `""` | qBittorrent WebUI password. Maps to subchart `settings.auth.pass` |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.auth.user | string | `""` | qBittorrent WebUI username. Maps to subchart `settings.auth.user` |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.config.baseUrl | string | `""` | qBittorrent base URL, e.g. https://qbit.example.org:443. Maps to subchart `settings.config.baseUrl` |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.config.insecureSkipVerify | bool | `false` | skip TLS certificate verification for the qBittorrent connection. Maps to subchart `settings.config.insecureSkipVerify` |
| tdarr-exporter.enabled | bool | `false` |  |
| tdarr-exporter.extraEnv | object | `{}` |  |
| tdarr-exporter.metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled | bool | `false` |  |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.apiKey | string | `""` |  |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.existingSecret.name | string | `""` |  |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.existingSecret.secretKey | string | `""` |  |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.log_level | string | `"info"` |  |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.url | string | `""` |  |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.verify_ssl | bool | `true` |  |

## Additional Exporters
This umbrella can optionally deploy two first-party exporters as subcharts. Configure each under its matching top-level key; every value in that subchart's own README applies verbatim under that key. The passthrough keys also appear in the [Configuration Options](#configuration-options) table above.

#### qbittorrent
Set `qbittorrent-exporter.enabled: true` to deploy it (wraps `martabal/qbittorrent-exporter`). Configure it under the `qbittorrent-exporter:` key â€” e.g. `qbittorrent-exporter.settings.config.baseUrl`. See the [qbittorrent-exporter chart README](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/qbittorrent-exporter) for the full value schema, and [Migrating to 3.0.0](#migrating-to-300) if upgrading from an earlier release.

#### Tdarr
Set `tdarr-exporter.enabled: true` to deploy it. Configure it under the `tdarr-exporter:` key â€” e.g. `tdarr-exporter.settings.config.url`. See the [tdarr-exporter chart README](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/tdarr-exporter) for the full value schema.

## Grafana Dashboards
A sample dashboard is available from upstream [onedr0p/exportarr](https://github.com/onedr0p/exportarr/tree/master/examples/grafana). A slightly modified version for this chart is shipped alongside it: [`examples/dashboard.json`](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/exportarr/examples/dashboard.json).
